items.reviewerNotes: Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: red battery/charging warning symbol and exact 4-option set (charge current/battery/ammeter/charging-circuit) match highPrecision q0584, correct text "charging circuit malfunction" = source option D.

Claude medium-risk keepUnresolved: screenshot shows a blue roundabout guide sign (Kreisverkehr symbol, correct answer A) but no candidate's option set matches the source set (roundabout/cross-intersection/multi-level-overpass/Y-intersection), confirmed by option-set-mismatch reason code.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot shows a four-bladed fan/blower symbol and q0597's option set matches exactly (correct "cooling or warming fan" = source A "blower with cold and warm air").

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot shows a key inside a car door (door-unlock symbol), matching q0624's correct answer "door unlock" = source option D "Türschloss aufschließen"; near-tie runner-up q0614 shares the option set but its child-safety-lock answer is ruled out by the image.
The source prompt is completely wrong compared to the screenshot. put this question in the next batch. and try again.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0531 is a near-verbatim ROW match on the headrest/rear-end-collision statement (score 38.8, far above unrelated runner-ups), correctRow W (false) maps to source option B) No.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q1005 "Smoking while driving cannot have an impact on safety" (ROW, correctRow W) is the same true/false claim as the source gloss that smoking is irrelevant to safety, and is the clear top score.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0212 is a near-verbatim paraphrase of the source (traffic police detaining accident vehicles to collect evidence), is highPrecision with no review flag and top score, and its "true" answer maps to A) Yes.

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot shows an open driver-view road with no lane markings and the exact "How to run on this road?" option set of q0415 (middle/both sides/by lanes/at will), with correct answer "in the middle of the road" mapping to source option B.

Claude high-risk keepUnresolved: image is a blue fork guide-sign with two diverging place-name arrows, but no candidate's option set cleanly matches the source four-way set (roundabout/cross/T/Y) and all scores are low (~46-58) with weak structural/option/gloss agreement, so no confident match.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot shows driver-view intersection with green light asking if you may continue after already passing the stop line, exactly matching q0636 (ROW, correctRow R=true), distinct from q0631's front-wheels variant; source A) Yes maps to the true answer.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0243 is a near-verbatim match (vehicle breakdown, warning sign 50m behind vehicle) with highPrecision:true and correctRow W=false, so the Yes/No statement is false = option B "No".

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: green guide sign with 出口/exit tag and an up-RIGHT diagonal arrow indicates a right expressway exit, matching q0883 (correct=highway right exit ahead) over the matcher's top q0824 (left exit), though the next/right/left/destination option family is shared across several masters.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0224 restates the same true/false scenario near-verbatim (no driving during deferred license checking due to military service or going abroad), is highPrecision, and clearly outranks the unrelated probation/suspension candidates; correctRow R.

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: q0197 is a near-exact paraphrase of the source ("driving in violation of regulations shall be subject to punishment"), correctRow R, though low total scores and a close-but-conceptually-distinct runner-up (q0323) add some uncertainty.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot shows a km/h dial with an odometer (888.8) = speed and mileage meter, exactly matching highPrecision candidate q0599's correct option A, whose meaning maps to source option B "speedometer and odometer".

Claude high-risk keepUnresolved: pre-fill proposed q0976 but adversarial check disagreed (q0976 is a plausible but not clearly-best match — runner-up q0046 ("observe dynamic situation of rear side vehicles before merging from other road") is an equally strong semantic near-duplicate with no source answer key or distinguishing options to break the tie (flagged runner-up-close/top2-near-duplicate/insufficient-evidence), though A=Yes correctly maps to q0976's "right/true" answer.); deferring to human.

Claude low-risk createNewQuestion: screenshot is the blue mandatory roundabout (Kreisverkehr) sign with three circular arrows and a "drive around the roundabout" option, but no candidate has a roundabout option set (candidates are one-way/turn arrow signs).

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0412 (highPrecision) is the same expressway/<50m-visibility/20km-h scenario with matching option set, and its correct answer "leave the highway" maps to source option B "leave the expressway".

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: image shows an inner-city street scene matching q0510's "city road" prompt with the exact same option set and order (A30/B40/C50/D70), and 50 km/h is the correct German innerstadt limit, though several near-duplicate candidates share the {30,40,50,70} set.

Claude high-risk keepUnresolved: screenshot shows a 120 Autobahn speed-limit sign so the top candidate q0367 (correct answer 90 km/h) is wrong, and the only answer-consistent candidate q0399 (120 km/h) scores low with malformed duplicate options and no image to confirm the same scene.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0394 is the same traffic-light-violation penalty-points question with an identical 2/3/6/12 option set ordered the same way (score 109.2 vs <31 for others), and its correct "6 point penalty" maps to source option C.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot shows driver-view pedestrian crossing a city street, matching highPrecision candidate q0355 "Stop and yield to the pedestrians under this situation" (correctRow R), so source Yes = A.

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: sign reads 下一出口 / 南京路 4 km (next exit ahead at distance), so the meaning is "highway next exit ahead" which uniquely matches q0673 (correctOptionText "highway next exit ahead", highPrecision:true), not the matcher's top q0824 whose stored answer is "left exit"; source option A "indication of the next expressway exit" is the answer letter.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: source "no trailer may be towed during probationary period" is a near-verbatim ROW match to q0342 "a driver is not allowed during probation to pull a trailer" (correctRow R), and the flagged negation-conflict is a false alarm since both express the same prohibition. [answer-key uncertain per check: q0342's prompt ("driver not allowed to pull a trailer during probation") genuinely matches the screenshot's German Yes/No statement, but the proposed answerKey "R" is the master row value, not a valid source option key — the correct source option is A (Yes), as confirmed by the parallel Spanish match (confirmedCorrectOptionKey="A").]

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0546 prompt "It lights when turning on the right-turn signal" exactly matches the German prompt and the green dual-arrow dashboard symbol (score 93.1, highPrecision-adjacent, requiresReview:false), with both arrows lit confirming correctRow W (false).

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: screenshot prompt "Wie kann man an dieser Kreuzung links abbiegen" and its four options (right/left of center, cannot turn left, at center) match q0519's left-turn-at-intersection option set exactly, with correct answer = left side of center = source option B.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: source scenario "hit-and-run that does not count as a crime" matches q0495's "escapes after causing a traffic accident but his conduct does not constitute a crime" verbatim (matcher's top q0981 is the wrong forged-license scenario), and its 12-point answer maps to source option A.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0647 (highPrecision, no review flag) "continuously flashing yellow light means the vehicle may speed up and pass" is the same assertion as the source "flashing yellow means one may drive fast" and is false, so the Yes/No answer is B) No.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: green Autobahn sign with yellow "60 km/h" plate is Zeichen 380 (recommended speed in poor weather), exactly matching highPrecision q0702 whose option set and correct answer "recommended speed in unusual/poor weather" map to source option B (vorgeschlagene Geschwindigkeit).
Owner: keep unresolved (2026-06-09). Entry/exit pair collision with 12.03.20 on q0802; #47 is likely the ENTRY variant (q0723) but deferred to de backlog for a later look rather than adjudicate now.

Claude high-risk keepUnresolved: source says "drive on the LEFT" while top candidate q0093 says "drive on the RIGHT" (correctRow R/true), a polarity-flipping discrepancy that makes the answer key indeterminate despite same mountain-road/slow-down/horn scenario.

Claude medium-risk approveExistingQid: image shows uphill driver-view with oncoming downhill car on a mountain slope, matching q0192's uphill-yields-to-downhill rule (correctRow R = true = "Yes" = source option A); slight wording gap ("if it has not begun going up") and q0192 highPrecision:false add mild uncertainty.

Claude low-risk approveExistingQid: q0024 (highPrecision, top score) is a near-exact paraphrase — icy/snow road plus light reflection causing visual disturbances/illusions — while all other candidates cover unrelated ice-driving topics (ABS, acceleration spins, engine braking, safe distance).

Claude high-risk keepUnresolved: screenshot shows a red-circle "120" max-speed sign on an expressway, but the answer to this image-dependent "minimum speed" question hinges on each candidate's own unseen reference image (q0410=90, q0505=100), so neither can be confidently matched nor an answer key derived.
